title: Disassemble and single-step a function
description: Disassemble a target function and step it in a debugger, reporting register, flag, and stack state against expectation.
body: Use when verifying or debugging low-level behavior. Actions: disassemble the function (objdump -d / debugger / Godbolt), set a breakpoint, single-step while watching the argument and callee-saved registers, flags, and the stack pointer/alignment, and report where the observed state diverges from expectation. Do not infer machine behavior from source alone. This skill exposes capability only and must not attach a debugger to a production or privileged process without runtime permission. Validation signals include stepped register/stack state matching the intended computation at each instruction. Risk boundary: if the binary lacks symbols or cannot be stepped in the available environment, report the behavior as unverified rather than asserting correctness from the mnemonics.
